What is one thing you learned today that you didn't know yesterday?
What is the most complex problem you solved this week, and how did you approach it?
What part of your codebase are you most proud of, and why?
What technical debt are you carrying right now, and what's your plan to address it?
Describe a recent bug you fixed. What was the root cause and what did you learn?
What is one tool or library you've been wanting to explore? What's stopping you?
What concept in software engineering do you feel least confident about? How could you strengthen it?
Describe your current project in three sentences. What is its core value proposition?
What would you do differently if you were starting your current project from scratch?
Who is a developer you admire and why? What can you learn from their approach?
What's a feature you cut corners on? What would the ideal implementation look like?
What's a non-technical skill (communication, time management, etc.) you're working on improving?
What is your biggest time-waster during a typical workday? How could you eliminate it?
Describe your ideal code review process. Does your current workflow match it?
What open-source project have you been meaning to contribute to? What's your first step?
How do you approach learning a new programming language or framework?
What does 'clean code' mean to you? Give a concrete example from your work.
What is a habit that makes you a better developer?
What would you tell your past self when you first started coding?
Describe a moment when you were stuck for a long time. How did you get unstuck?
What's a recent article, video, or book that changed how you think about software?
How do you balance writing perfect code vs. shipping on time?
What's the most elegant algorithm or data structure you've used recently?
How has your definition of 'good software' changed over time?
What's a project idea you've had but never built? What's the core technical challenge?
How do you handle imposter syndrome or self-doubt in your technical work?
What testing strategies do you use? What would you improve about your current approach?
What part of your development environment or setup has made the biggest difference in your productivity?
How do you keep up with new technologies without feeling overwhelmed?
What is a principle or philosophy (e.g., SOLID, DRY, YAGNI) that guides your daily work?
What's one refactoring you've been putting off? Break it into three small steps.
Describe the architecture of a system you find impressive. What makes it elegant?
How do you explain a complex technical concept to a non-technical stakeholder?
What metrics do you use to measure the quality of your code or your team's velocity?
What is the most important lesson you've learned from a failed project?
